How to read a painting.

Most visitors read the label and move on. These questions describe how art historians inspect a painting before interpreting it.

The eight questions

The order matters: look first and interpret later.

1what is thisthe name, the painter, the year. One sentence, out loud.
2first looklist what is there before deciding what any of it means.
3who is herecount everyone: people, animals, reflections, and backs of heads.
4what is happeninggive one sentence you could defend while pointing at the paint.

Nine paintings, read closely

Each reading takes about three minutes, and the camera points to the relevant details.
